BillingStartTime.name: startTime
BillingStartTime.description: Start of the billing period (RFC 3339). Defaults to 30 days ago. Snapped down to the start of its bucketSize bucket so the window aligns with the returned records; provide a boundary-aligned value (e.g. midnight for bucketSize=day) to avoid widening.

BillingEndTime.name: endTime
BillingEndTime.description: End of the billing period (RFC 3339), exclusive. Defaults to now. Snapped up to the end of the bucketSize bucket it lands in (unless already on a boundary) so the window aligns with the returned records.

BillingBucketSize.name: bucketSize
BillingBucketSize.description: Length of each billing time bucket. Defaults to day.

BillingLastN.name: lastN
BillingLastN.description: Return the last N buckets of bucketSize, ending with the current (in-progress) bucket — e.g. lastN=100 with bucketSize=day is "last 100 days". The resolved window is aligned to bucket boundaries: startTime is the start of the earliest bucket (e.g. midnight of the earliest day) and endTime is the end of the current bucket. Mutually exclusive with startTime/endTime; provide one or the other, not both.

CatalogIncludeParam.name: include
CatalogIncludeParam.description: Comma-separated optional expansions. Supported value today: AVAILABILITY. This may expand with more include values in the future.

GpuProductFilter.name: product
GpuProductFilter.description: Comma-separated availability product contexts. Supported values: POD, CLUSTER, SERVERLESS. Valid only with include=AVAILABILITY. Upstream default when omitted: POD.

GpuCountFilter.name: count
GpuCountFilter.description: GPU count for availability and lowest-price calculations. Valid only with include=AVAILABILITY. Defaults to 1.

GpuCloudFilter.name: cloud
GpuCloudFilter.description: Cloud type for availability and lowest-price calculations. Valid only with include=AVAILABILITY. Supported values: SECURE, COMMUNITY. Upstream default when omitted: SECURE.

MinCudaVersionFilter.name: minCudaVersion
MinCudaVersionFilter.description: Minimum CUDA version for availability and lowest-price calculations. Valid only with include=AVAILABILITY. Format: integer major or major.minor, e.g. 12 or 12.1.

CpuProductFilter.name: product
CpuProductFilter.description: Comma-separated availability product context. Valid only with include=AVAILABILITY. Supported values for CPUs: POD, SERVERLESS. Defaults to POD when omitted.

CpuVCPUCountFilter.name: vcpuCount
CpuVCPUCountFilter.description: Availability vCPU count. Valid only with include=AVAILABILITY. Must be a power of two.

LogSourceParam.name: source
LogSourceParam.description: Log source to stream. Omit to include both container and system logs.

LogTail.name: tail
LogTail.description:
Number of historical lines to backfill before streaming. Defaults to
100 when omitted; set `0` to stream live with no backfill. Maximum 5000.
Ignored when `since` or `Last-Event-ID` is provided — the resume cursor
drives the backfill instead.

LogSince.name: since
LogSince.description:
RFC3339 timestamp to resume from. Ignored when `Last-Event-ID` is
provided. When set, the stream resumes from this point and `tail` is
ignored.

LogLastEventID.name: Last-Event-ID
LogLastEventID.description:
SSE reconnect cursor — a timestamp emitted by this endpoint. Takes
precedence over `since` and `tail`: when present, the stream resumes
from this point. Sent automatically by EventSource on reconnect.

ContainerConfig.description: Reusable container configuration shared across templates, pods, and serverless endpoints. Adding a field here automatically propagates to all three resources.

args.description: Arguments passed to the container entrypoint

disk.description: Container disk in GB (ephemeral, wiped on restart)

registry.description: Container registry credential ID (for private images)

Mounts.description:
Storage mounts attached to a pod. At-most-one of `persistent` or
`network` may be set today (mutually exclusive, enforced at the
handler with 400 if both are present). The `network` field is an
array for forward compatibility with eventual multi-network-volume
support, but `maxItems` is 1 today.

PATCH semantics:
- Omitting `mounts` or sending `{}` leaves the existing mount
  unchanged.
- An explicit `network: []` is rejected with 400 (clearing mounts
  is not supported).
- Mount kind is fixed at create — a PATCH that introduces a kind
  not present at create (persistent on a network pod, network on
  a persistent pod, or any mount on a previously-mountless pod)
  is rejected with 400.
- The `volumeId` of a network mount is immutable; a PATCH that
  names a different `volumeId` is rejected with 400.
- Partial mounts are not supported — every mount entry must
  include the full schema (`size` + `path` for persistent,
  `volumeId` + `path` for network). Missing required fields → 422.

TemplateMounts.description:
Storage mounts attached to a template. Templates support only a
single persistent mount today; any `network` property is rejected
with 422 by the schema validator.

PATCH semantics: omitting `mounts` or sending `{}` leaves the
existing mount unchanged.

PersistentMount.description:
Host-local persistent storage. Pinned to the pod's host machine — data
does not survive a host failure. Disallowed on CPU pods. Mutually
exclusive with NetworkMount. Deprecated: prefer NetworkMount for any
data you cannot recreate.

size.description: Host-local persistent storage in GB. Upstream enforces a 10 GB floor.

path.description: Mount path inside the container. May be changed via PATCH.

NetworkMount.description:
Reference to a NetworkVolume. Custom paths are honored at runtime on
both GPU and CPU pods. The underlying `volumeId` is immutable
post-create; the mount `path` may be changed via PATCH.

volumeId.description: ID of an existing NetworkVolume in the same data center as the pod.

path.description: Mount path inside the container. No default — must be specified explicitly.

id.description: CPU flavor identifier, as returned by GET /v2/catalog/cpus.

vcpuCount.description: Number of vCPUs. Must be valid for the selected CPU flavor and must be a power of two.

memory.description: Memory allocated to the pod in GB.

ScalerType.description:
Autoscaling strategy.
- `QUEUE_DELAY`   — scale on seconds a request waits in queue
- `REQUEST_COUNT` — scale on in-flight request count

FlashBoot.description:
FlashBoot cold-start acceleration mode.
- `OFF`                — disabled
- `FLASHBOOT`          — enabled
- `PRIORITY_FLASHBOOT` — enabled with priority capacity

pools.description:
Serverless GPU pool IDs (as returned by `GET /v2/catalog/gpus` in
`pool`). Workers are placed on whichever listed pool has capacity.

cpu.description: Read-only. Present for CPU serverless endpoints; CPU create/update is not yet supported.

idleTimeout.description: Seconds before idle workers scale down

WorkerStatus.description:
Derived worker state, reconciled from the worker pod's lifecycle status
and the live job-queue view.
- `RUNNING`      — actively processing a job
- `IDLE`         — ready and polling for jobs
- `INITIALIZING` — starting up, not yet ready
- `THROTTLED`    — waiting on compute capacity
- `UNHEALTHY`    — failing health checks

isStale.description:
True when the worker is running an older endpoint configuration than
the current one (e.g. mid rolling-update). This is the authoritative
flag: it is derived from `version` vs the response's `endpointVersion`,
except on legacy endpoints (`endpointVersion` 1) where it falls back
to a container-image comparison.

version.description:
Endpoint configuration version this worker is running. Compare with
the response's `endpointVersion`. Null if unknown.

image.description: Container image the worker is running.

uptimeSeconds.description: Seconds the worker has been running. Null until the worker is placed and running.

gpuTypeId.description: GPU type the worker is placed on. Null until the worker is placed.

dataCenterId.description: Data center the worker is placed in. Null until the worker is placed.

startedAt.description: When the worker last started. Null if it has not started.

WorkerSummary.description:
Histogram of the returned workers by status. The per-status counts are a
roll-up of the `workers` array, so `running + idle + initializing +
throttled + unhealthy == total == len(workers)`.

initializing.description: Workers starting up, not yet ready.

total.description: All workers currently allocated to the endpoint.

endpointVersion.description:
The endpoint's current configuration version. A worker whose
`version` differs is running stale config (see `worker.isStale`).
Null if unknown.

ReleaseSource.description:
What produced the release.
- `GIT_BUILD` — a completed GitHub build (see `buildId`)
- `MANUAL`    — a manual configuration change

field.description:
The changed configuration field. Top-level (e.g. `gpuCount`,
`locations`) or template-scoped (e.g. `template.imageName`,
`template.env`).

old.description: Previous value, as raw JSON. Null when the field was added.

new.description: New value, as raw JSON. Null when the field was removed.

version.description: The endpoint configuration version this release produced.

buildId.description:
The GitHub build that produced this release. Set when `source` is
`GIT_BUILD`; null for `MANUAL` releases. Fetch build detail/logs via
`/v2/serverless/{id}/builds/{buildId}`.

createdByUserId.description: ID of the user who created the release.

workerCount.description: Workers currently running this release's version.

diff.description: Configuration fields that changed in this release.

inProgress.description: True while any worker is still running an older version.

workersOnLatest.description: Workers running the endpoint's current version.

workersTotal.description: All workers currently allocated to the endpoint.

percentOnLatest.description: Percentage of workers on the current version (0 when there are no workers).

endpointVersion.description: The endpoint's current configuration version. Null if unknown.

dataCenterIds.description: Preferred data centers for placement. Omit or pass an empty array to let the scheduler choose.
Preferred data centers for placement. Omit or pass an empty array to let the scheduler choose.

Utilization.description: Single-value utilization percentage (0–100). Shared by `cpu` and `memory`.

PodRuntimePort.description: Live port mapping for a running pod.

PodRuntime.description: Live utilization metrics for a running pod.

PodStatus.description:
Lifecycle status of a pod.
- `PROVISIONING` — pod is being allocated
- `STARTING`     — container is starting
- `RUNNING`      — container is healthy
- `EXITED`       — container exited (stopped)
- `ERROR`        — container is in an unrecoverable error state
- `TERMINATED`   — pod has been permanently deleted

PodAction.description: State transition to trigger on a pod.

LogSource.description:
Log source to include in a log stream.
- `container` — container stdout/stderr log stream
- `system`    — host lifecycle/userlogs stream

Cloud.description:
Cloud tier.
- `SECURE`    — RunPod-owned datacenter hardware
- `COMMUNITY` — community-hosted hardware
- `ALL`       — eligible for either tier (request-only; responses always
  report the concrete tier the pod was placed on)

TemplateCategory.description:
Hardware family this template targets.
- `CPU`    — CPU-only workloads
- `NVIDIA` — NVIDIA GPU workloads
- `AMD`    — AMD GPU workloads

enabled.description: Whether global networking is enabled, giving the pod a private IP reachable across data centers. Derived from whether the pod has an assigned global-network address.

ip.description: The pod's assigned global-networking IP. Present only when enabled.

internalDns.description: Internal DNS name (`<podId>.runpod.internal`), reachable from other globally-networked pods in the same account. Present only when enabled.

actions.description: Valid state transitions for the current status.

gpu.description: Present for GPU pods; omitted from CPU pods.

cpu.description: Present for CPU pods; omitted from GPU pods.

dataCenterId.description: Data center where the pod is running (assigned by scheduler)

template.description: ID of the template this pod was created from

cost.description: Current cost in USD per hour (0.0 when EXITED or TERMINATED)

locked.description: Whether the pod is locked (prevents stopping or resetting)

runtime.description: Live utilization metrics. Null when the pod is not RUNNING.

allOf.description:
Request body for creating a pod. Exactly one of `gpu` or `cpu`
must be set — enforced at the handler layer. For CPU pods, memory
is derived by the API from the selected flavor's RAM multiplier;
clients provide only CPU flavor and vCPU count. CPU pods support
container disk and network volumes only; `mounts.persistent` is
invalid when `cpu` is set.

dataCenterIds.description:
Preferred data centers for placement. Omit or pass an empty
array to let the scheduler choose.

globalNetworking.description: Enable global networking, giving the pod a private IP reachable across data centers. Requires an NVIDIA GPU and a global-networking-enabled data center (both enforced upstream). See `GET /v2/catalog/datacenters` (`globalNetwork`) for eligible data centers.

locked.description: Lock the pod (true) or unlock it (false). Locked pods cannot be stopped or reset.

globalNetworking.description: Enable (true) or disable (false) global networking. Takes effect on the next pod start/restart, not live. Requires an NVIDIA GPU and a global-networking-enabled data center (both enforced upstream). See `GET /v2/catalog/datacenters` (`globalNetwork`) for eligible data centers.

serverless.description: Whether this template is for serverless workers (true) or pods (false)

public.description: Whether this template is visible to other RunPod users

TagResourceType.description: Resource type attached to a tag.

resources.description: Populated only on direct tag responses when `?include=resources` is provided; omitted when the tag appears nested in another resource or if no resources have the tag.
Resources to attach to the tag at create time. If any resource cannot be attached, tag creation returns an error rather than silently dropping the association.

UpdateTagRequest.description: At least one of `key`, `value`, or `resources` must be provided.

resources.description:
Replaces resource associations when present. Omit `resources` to leave
existing associations unchanged. Send `resources: []` to remove all
associations. Send a non-empty array to replace current associations with
exactly those resources.

name.description: Human-readable name (not required to be unique)

dataCenter.description: Data center location; immutable after creation

type.description: Storage tier of this volume. Set at creation and immutable.

tags.description: Tags attached to this network volume.

dataCenter.description: Data center in which to create the volume

type.description:
Storage tier for the volume. Optional. When omitted, the volume is
provisioned using the requested data center's default (primary)
storage tier. HIGH_PERFORMANCE provisions a high-performance (HPS)
volume; STANDARD provisions a standard volume. A volume's tier is
immutable after creation.

UpdateNetworkVolumeRequest.description:
Only the provided fields are updated. At least one field must be
present; an empty body is rejected.

size.description:
New size in GB. Must be greater than or equal to the current size —
network volume storage cannot be reduced.

username.description: Registry username (write-only, not returned in responses)

password.description: Registry password (write-only, not returned in responses)

id.description: Individual GPU type identifier (use for pod creation)

pool.description: Serverless GPU pool ID (use for serverless endpoint creation). Null if GPU is not in a serverless pool.

availability.description: Overall GPU availability. Present only when requested with include=AVAILABILITY.

dataCenters.description: Per-datacenter GPU availability. Present only when requested with include=AVAILABILITY.

CatalogInclude.description: Catalog include expansion. Only AVAILABILITY is supported today; additional include values may be added in the future.

VolumeType.description: Data center network volume storage type.

id.description: CPU flavor identifier (use in cpu.id for pod creation)

ramGbPerVcpu.description:
GB of RAM allocated per vCPU. Multiply by the chosen vCPU
count (within `vcpu.min`..`vcpu.max`) to get the total RAM
for an instance of this flavor. May be fractional.

securePerVcpu.description:
Price for secure pods per vCPU. Multiply by the chosen vCPU
count (within `vcpu.min`..`vcpu.max`) to get the total price.

serverlessPerVcpu.description:
Price for serverless per vCPU. Multiply by the chosen vCPU
count (within `vcpu.min`..`vcpu.max`) to get the total price.

availability.description: Overall CPU availability. Present only when requested with include=AVAILABILITY.

dataCenters.description: Per-datacenter CPU availability. Present only when requested with include=AVAILABILITY.

globalNetwork.description: Whether this data center supports global networking (private cross-datacenter pod-to-pod network).

networkVolumeTypes.description: Network volume tiers this DC supports. Empty = none.

compliance.description: Compliance certifications held by this data center

gpuAvailability.description: Per-GPU availability in this data center. Present only when requested with include=gpuAvailability.

cpuAvailability.description: Per-CPU availability in this data center. Present only when requested with include=cpuAvailability.

DataCenterRegion.description: Continental region containing the data center.

BillingTimeRange.description: Half-open time range [startTime, endTime) in RFC 3339. On a record it is the time bucket; on a query echo it is the resolved window.

BillingBucketSize.description: Length of each billing time bucket.

BillingAmounts.description: Total spend across all billable RunPod resources with each cost component broken out, fully prefixed by resource. Backs the aggregate record's amounts and the metadata totals.

totalAmount.description: Total cost in USD for the bucket across all resources.

podGpuAmount.description: GPU pod compute cost in USD for the bucket.

podCpuAmount.description: CPU pod compute cost in USD for the bucket.

podDiskAmount.description: Pod disk cost in USD for the bucket.

serverlessGpuAmount.description: Serverless GPU compute cost in USD for the bucket.

serverlessCpuAmount.description: Serverless CPU compute cost in USD for the bucket.

serverlessDiskAmount.description: Serverless disk cost in USD for the bucket.

serverlessFeeAmount.description: Serverless platform fee in USD for the bucket.

storageStandardAmount.description: Standard network volume storage cost in USD for the bucket.

storageHighPerformanceAmount.description: High-performance network volume storage cost in USD for the bucket.

endpointAmount.description: RunPod public endpoint cost in USD for the bucket.

clusterGpuAmount.description: Instant Cluster GPU compute cost in USD for the bucket.

clusterDiskAmount.description: Instant Cluster disk cost in USD for the bucket.

clusterNetworkingAmount.description: Instant Cluster inter-node networking cost in USD for the bucket.

BillingRecord.description: A single time-bucketed record of total spend across all billable RunPod resources, with each cost component broken out. Returned by GET /v2/billing.

PodBillingAmounts.description: Pod cost components covering both GPU and CPU pods. Backs a record's amounts and the metadata totals.

totalAmount.description: Total pod cost in USD for the bucket.

gpuAmount.description: GPU pod compute cost in USD for the bucket.

cpuAmount.description: CPU pod compute cost in USD for the bucket.

diskAmount.description: Pod disk cost in USD for the bucket.

PodBillingRecord.description: A single time-bucketed pod billing record, covering both GPU and CPU pods. Returned by GET /v2/billing/pods.

podId.description: The pod this record bills. When the podId filter is set every record carries that id; otherwise one record is emitted per pod per bucket.

ServerlessBillingAmounts.description: Serverless cost components. Backs a record's amounts and the metadata totals.

totalAmount.description: Total serverless cost in USD for the bucket.

gpuAmount.description: Serverless GPU compute cost in USD for the bucket.

cpuAmount.description: Serverless CPU compute cost in USD for the bucket.

diskAmount.description: Serverless disk cost in USD for the bucket.

feeAmount.description: Serverless platform fee in USD for the bucket.

ServerlessBillingRecord.description: A single time-bucketed serverless billing record. Returned by GET /v2/billing/serverless.

serverlessId.description: The serverless endpoint this record bills. When the serverlessId filter is set every record carries that id; otherwise one record is emitted per serverless endpoint per bucket.

EndpointBillingAmounts.description: RunPod public endpoint cost. Backs a record's amounts and the metadata totals.

totalAmount.description: Total public endpoint cost in USD for the bucket.

EndpointBillingRecord.description: A single time-bucketed RunPod public endpoint billing record. Returned by GET /v2/billing/endpoints.

NetworkVolumeBillingAmounts.description: Network volume storage cost, split into standard and high-performance. Backs a record's amounts and the metadata totals.

totalAmount.description: Total network volume cost in USD for the bucket, across standard and high-performance storage.

standardAmount.description: Standard storage cost in USD for the bucket.

highPerformanceAmount.description: High-performance storage cost in USD for the bucket.

NetworkVolumeBillingRecord.description: A single time-bucketed network volume billing record, split into standard and high-performance storage. Returned by GET /v2/billing/networkvolumes.

networkVolumeId.description: The network volume this record bills. When the networkVolumeId filter is set every record carries that id; otherwise one record is emitted per network volume per bucket.

ClusterBillingAmounts.description: Instant Cluster cost components (GPU-only, no CPU). Backs a record's amounts and the metadata totals.

totalAmount.description: Total Instant Cluster cost in USD for the bucket.

gpuAmount.description: Cluster GPU compute cost in USD for the bucket.

diskAmount.description: Cluster disk cost in USD for the bucket.

networkingAmount.description: Cluster inter-node networking cost in USD for the bucket.

ClusterBillingRecord.description: A single time-bucketed Instant Cluster billing record. Clusters are GPU-only (no CPU component). Returned by GET /v2/billing/clusters.

clusterId.description: The Instant Cluster this record bills. When the clusterId filter is set every record carries that id; otherwise one record is emitted per cluster per bucket.

BillingQuery.description: Resolved query window and granularity (routes without a filter).

podId.description: The podId filter applied, if any.

serverlessId.description: The serverlessId filter applied, if any.

networkVolumeId.description: The networkVolumeId filter applied, if any.

clusterId.description: The clusterId filter applied, if any.

recordCount.description: Number of records returned (buckets times distinct pods).

uniquePodCount.description: Number of distinct pods the records span.

recordCount.description: Number of records returned (buckets times distinct endpoints).

uniqueServerlessCount.description: Number of distinct serverless endpoints the records span.

recordCount.description: Number of records returned (buckets times distinct volumes).

uniqueNetworkVolumeCount.description: Number of distinct network volumes the records span.

recordCount.description: Number of records returned (buckets times distinct clusters).

uniqueClusterCount.description: Number of distinct clusters the records span.

ListBillingResponse.description: Aggregated billing records across all RunPod resources.

ListEndpointBillingResponse.description: Billing records for RunPod public endpoints.

get.description: Returns all pods owned by the authenticated user.

post.description:
Creates a new pod. Specify `gpu` for compute.
ContainerConfig fields can be spread from a template response.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Pod identifier

get.description: Returns a single pod by ID.

patch.description: Updates pod configuration. Only provided fields are changed.

delete.description: Permanently terminates and deletes a pod.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Pod identifier

get.description:
Streams pod logs as Server-Sent Events. The `source` query parameter
selects which log source to include.

The SSE `data` payload shape is:
`{ "source": "container", "line": "...", "ts": "..." }`.
Log-event `id` values are the event `ts` timestamp so
browser/EventSource reconnects can resume with `Last-Event-ID`.

Connection.description: Always `keep-alive` to hold the SSE stream open.

X-Accel-Buffering.description: Always `no` to disable proxy buffering of the SSE stream.

post.description:
Triggers a state transition on a pod. Valid actions depend on the current status
and are listed in the pod's `actions` field.

409.description: Action not valid for current pod status

get.description: Returns all serverless endpoints owned by the authenticated user.

post.description:
Creates a serverless endpoint. Specify `gpu` for compute (CPU serverless
endpoints are read-only). ContainerConfig fields can be spread from a
template response.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Serverless endpoint identifier

get.description: Returns a single serverless endpoint by ID.

patch.description: Updates serverless endpoint configuration. Only provided fields are changed.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a serverless endpoint and its bound template.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Serverless endpoint identifier

get.description:
Returns the active workers for a serverless endpoint plus a summary of
worker counts. Each worker's `status` is derived by reconciling the
worker pod's lifecycle status with the endpoint's live job-queue view.
When the job-queue view is unavailable the response degrades to the
pod-derived status and counts.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Serverless endpoint identifier

get.description:
Returns the endpoint's release history (newest first) plus a rollout
summary of how many workers are running the current version. Each
release is a versioned configuration snapshot with a `diff` of what
changed; build-driven releases carry a `buildId` (fetch build detail via
the builds sub-routes).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Serverless endpoint identifier

parameters.name: workerId
parameters.description: Worker identifier (from GET /v2/serverless/{id}/workers)

get.description:
Streams a serverless worker's logs as Server-Sent Events. The `source`
query parameter selects which log source to include.

The SSE `data` payload shape is:
`{ "source": "container", "line": "...", "ts": "..." }`.
Log-event `id` values are the event `ts` timestamp so browser/EventSource
reconnects can resume with `Last-Event-ID`.

Connection.description: Always `keep-alive` to hold the SSE stream open.

X-Accel-Buffering.description: Always `no` to disable proxy buffering of the SSE stream.

get.description: Returns all templates owned by the authenticated user.

patch.description: Updates template configuration. Only provided fields are changed.

get.description: Returns all tags owned by the authenticated user.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Comma-separated optional expansions. Supported value: `resources`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Tag identifier

get.description: Returns a single tag by ID.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Comma-separated optional expansions. Supported value: `resources`.

patch.description:
Updates a tag. At least one of `key`, `value`, or `resources` must be provided.

When `resources` is provided, it replaces the tag's complete resource association set. Resource replacement is not atomic: the API creates missing associations first, then deletes stale associations using separate upstream operations. If an upstream operation fails after earlier operations have succeeded, the response is an error and the tag may contain a partially-applied resource set. Retrying the same request with the same `resources` array is safe and will attempt to converge the tag to the requested set. Use the explicit tag-resource attach/detach operations when you need smaller idempotent association changes.

422.description: Unprocessable request body (missing one of `key`, `value`, or `resources`)

delete.description: Deletes a tag. This operation is idempotent; deleting a tag that does not exist returns `204`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Tag identifier

parameters.name: resourceType
parameters.description: Resource type to attach or detach.

parameters.name: resourceId
parameters.description: Resource identifier to attach or detach.

put.summary: Attach a resource to a tag
put.description: Ensures the resource is attached to the tag. This operation is idempotent; attaching an already-associated resource returns `204`.

delete.summary: Detach a resource from a tag
delete.description: Ensures the resource is detached from the tag. This operation is idempotent; detaching a resource that is not associated with the tag returns `204`.

get.description: Returns all network volumes owned by the authenticated user.

post.description: Provisions a new network volume in the specified data center.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Network volume identifier

get.description: Returns a single network volume by ID.

patch.description:
Updates mutable fields on a network volume. Only provided fields are changed.
Note: `size` may only increase; attempts to reduce size will be rejected.

400.description: Invalid request body or size decrease attempted

delete.description: Permanently deletes a network volume and releases its storage.

get.description: Returns all container registry credentials owned by the authenticated user.

post.description: Stores credentials for a private container registry. Credentials are write-only.

get.description: Returns available GPU types with pricing. Availability is included only when requested with include=AVAILABILITY.
Returns a single GPU type with pricing. Availability details are included only when requested with include=AVAILABILITY.
Returns available CPU flavors. Availability is included only when requested with include=AVAILABILITY.
Returns a single CPU type with pricing. Availability details are included only when requested with include=AVAILABILITY.
Returns available data center locations.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Comma-separated optional expansions. Supported value: GPU_AVAILABILITY, CPU_AVAILABILITY.

parameters.name: regions
parameters.description: Comma-separated DataCenterRegion enum values. Values within this filter use OR semantics. Different filter families combine with AND.

parameters.name: networkVolumeTypes
parameters.description: Comma-separated volume types. Supported values: STANDARD, HIGH_PERFORMANCE. Values within this filter use AND semantics; volumes=STANDARD,HIGH_PERFORMANCE requires both storage types. Different filter families combine with AND.

parameters.name: compliance
parameters.description: Comma-separated Compliance enum values. Values within this filter use AND semantics; compliance=GDPR,SOC_2_TYPE_2 requires both certifications. Different filter families combine with AND.

parameters.name: globalNetwork
parameters.description: Filter by global networking support. true returns only data centers that support global networking; false only those that do not. Different filter families combine with AND.

get.description: Returns a single data center. Availability is included only when requested with include=GPU_AVAILABILITY or include=CPU_AVAILABILITY.

parameters.name: include
parameters.description: Comma-separated optional expansions. Supported value: GPU_AVAILABILITY, CPU_AVAILABILITY.

get.description: Returns time-bucketed total spend across all billable RunPod resources for the authenticated user over the requested period, with each cost component broken out.
Returns time-bucketed pod billing records (GPU and CPU pods) for the authenticated user over the requested period.

parameters.name: podId
